title: Pivot or Persevere

content.statement: Decide regularly whether to change strategy or double down.
content.question: What criteria would tell me to pivot rather than persevere, and have I written them down before the data comes in?
content.equation: Pivot Quality = Data Honesty × Pre-Set Criteria × Decision Speed
content.mental_model: Fork in the road, keep walking or take the new path.
content.business_lens: Set quarterly pivot criteria in advance; review honestly; pivot one variable at a time.
content.life_lens: Define in advance what would make you change course so emotion does not run the decision.
sop: Set criteria for pivot vs persevere before the cycle
Pivot only one element (segment, feature, channel, pricing)
content.explainer: Twitter began as Odeo (podcast platform). When podcasting stalled, the team pivoted to short status updates. Pivot is not failure, it is intelligent redirection of validated learning. The danger is unprincipled pivoting that throws away every lesson; one variable at a time is the discipline.
questions: What criteria would tell me to pivot rather than persevere, and have I written them down before the data comes in?
The last time you almost pivoted, was the deciding factor real evidence, or how tired and discouraged you felt that week?
If you changed exactly one variable, segment, price, channel, or feature, which one would you bet resolves the most uncertainty fastest?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Data Honesty 8, Pre-Set Criteria 6, Decision Speed 7. Score = (8 plus 6 plus 7) divided by 3 = 7.0. Higher means your pivot decisions are evidence-driven and fast; lower means you're deciding on mood, after the fact, or too slowly for the decision to matter.
content.closing_statement: Advance criteria protect you from your own hope.

caption:
Principle Nº 291: Pivot or Persevere

Decide regularly whether to change strategy or double down. Twitter began as Odeo (podcast platform).

Ask yourself: What criteria would tell me to pivot rather than persevere, and have I written them down before the data comes in?
